title: Work in Canada temporarily – fee exemptions - Canada.ca
content:
## Work in Canada temporarily – fee exemptions
In most cases, workers and employers must pay fees before we can issue a work permit. Check the fee list for the most up-to-date application fees.
In rare situations, you or your employer may be exempt from certain fees. Check the tables below to see the fee exemptions for certain types of:
- employer-specific work permits
If your situation isn’t described below, it means you and your employer are not exempt from paying fees .

## Fee exemptions for employer-specific work permits
The employer is responsible for the employer compliance fee .
Exempt from work permit processing fee
Employer is exempt from employer compliance fee
Workers participating in the Fulbright Program (Canada-U.S.)
Workers at Roosevelt Campobello International Park
US Government Personnel working in Canada on official US Government business
Someone working under a cultural agreement between Canada and Belgium, Brazil, People’s Republic of China, France, Germany, Italy, Japan or Mexico
Family members of military personnel who are working under a reciprocal agreement
Family members of accredited foreign missions workers
Workers at International Development Research Centre (IDRC)
Sponsored distinguished scientists or post-doctoral fellows at Atomic Energy of Canada Ltd (AECL)
Sponsored distinguished scientists or scholars at National Research Council of Canada (NRC) or Natural Sciences and Engineering Research Council of Canada (NSERC)
Research Chair holders, such as Canada Research Chair (CRC) and Canada Excellence Research chair positions (CERC)
Charitable or religious workers and organizations (only if the worker will not be paid for their work)

## Fee exemptions for open work permits
Exempt from work permit processing fee
Exempt from open work permit fee
Refugee claimants who need to work to support themselves
Failed refugee claimants or others whose removal order can’t be enforced and who need to support themselves
Members of the Convention refugees abroad class or members of a humanitarian-protected persons abroad class
Family members of members of the Convention refugees abroad class or of a humanitarian-protected persons abroad class
Persons who have received stage-1 approval on an application for permanent residence on humanitarian and compassionate grounds or based on public policy considerations
Family members of permanent residence applicants who have received stage-1 approval on humanitarian and compassionate grounds or based on public policy considerations
Protected persons (permanent resident applicants in Canada)
Family members of protected persons
Individuals who have an unenforceable removal order who need to support themselves
Temporary Resident Permit holders
Victims of family violence who hold a temporary resident permit for at least 180 days
Live-in caregivers who have met the work requirement of 3900 hours (or 2 years) and have applied or are applying for permanent residence
Family members of someone in the Live-in Caregiver Class in Canada who have applied for permanent residence and whose application has been approved in principle
Family members of U.S. Government personnel who are working in Canada on official US Government business
Family members of military personnel who are working under a reciprocal agreement
Family members of accredited foreign missions workers

## Fee exemptions for co-op work permits
Exempt from work permit processing fee
Exempt from open work permit fee
Participants in a postsecondary academic, vocational or professional training program offered by a designated learning institution in Canada
Participants in a secondary program requiring work (includes vocational training programs offered by a designated learning institution in Quebec)
